questions.text: Which organ helps us to digest food?
questions.explanation: The stomach helps digest the food we eat.

questions.text: How many bones does the human skeleton have (adult)?
questions.explanation: An adult human body has 206 bones.

questions.text: We breathe in oxygen and breathe out ____.
questions.explanation: We breathe out carbon dioxide.

questions.text: Milk teeth fall out and are replaced by permanent teeth.
questions.explanation: Milk teeth are replaced by permanent teeth. True.

questions.text: Which part of the plant makes seeds?
questions.explanation: Flowers make fruits and seeds.

questions.text: Green leaves contain a substance called ____ which helps make food.
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll (green colour) helps plants make food.

questions.text: Desert plants like cactus store water in their stems.
questions.explanation: Cactus stores water in its thick stem. True.

questions.text: Animals that lay eggs are called ____.
questions.explanation: Egg-laying animals are oviparous (e.g., hen, frog).

questions.text: Animals that give birth to babies are called ____.
questions.explanation: Animals that give birth to young ones are viviparous (e.g., cow, human).

questions.text: A spider has eight legs.
questions.explanation: A spider has 8 legs. True.

questions.text: Which nutrient helps build muscles?
questions.explanation: Proteins help build and repair muscles.

questions.text: Oranges and lemons are rich in vitamin ____.
questions.explanation: Citrus fruits like orange and lemon are rich in vitamin C.

questions.text: Food kept open for long can get spoiled.
questions.explanation: Open food spoils due to germs. True.

questions.text: The process by which water turns into vapour is called ____.
questions.explanation: Water turning into vapour is evaporation.

questions.text: Water vapour cools and forms clouds — this is called ____.
questions.explanation: Vapour cooling into droplets is condensation.

questions.text: The water cycle has evaporation, condensation and rainfall.
questions.explanation: These are stages of the water cycle. True.

questions.text: Which gas in air do plants use to make food?
questions.explanation: Plants use carbon dioxide to make food.

questions.text: A ____ measures temperature.
questions.explanation: A thermometer measures temperature.

questions.text: Wind is moving air.
questions.explanation: Wind is air in motion. True.

questions.text: Which of these is a natural resource?
questions.explanation: Water is a natural resource given by nature.

questions.text: Cutting down forests is called ____.
questions.explanation: Cutting forests is deforestation.

questions.text: Saving electricity helps protect the environment.
questions.explanation: Saving energy reduces pollution. True.

questions.text: Which invention made travel on roads faster long ago?
questions.explanation: The invention of the wheel made road travel faster.

questions.text: Communication using letters sent by post is called ____ mail.
questions.explanation: Letters sent by post are postal mail.

questions.text: Wearing a seat belt in a car keeps us safe.
questions.explanation: Seat belts protect us in a car. True.

questions.text: Which direction does the Sun rise from?
questions.explanation: The Sun rises in the east.

questions.text: A ____ shows directions like North, South, East and West.
questions.explanation: A compass shows directions.

questions.text: Roots usually grow downward into the soil.
questions.explanation: Roots grow down into the soil. True.

questions.text: Which sense organ is used for tasting?
questions.explanation: We taste with our tongue.

questions.text: A balanced diet should include proteins, carbohydrates, fats, vitamins and minerals.
questions.explanation: A balanced diet has all these nutrients. True.

questions.text: Which instrument shows wind direction?
questions.explanation: A weather vane shows the direction of the wind.
